nba.note: Celtics close a 3-game homestand sweep — Tatum 24/10 in blowout of Warriors

games.tag: SO · Stars within 2 of Central lead

nhl.note: Six-game Wednesday slate — Stars 15-1-1 in last 17, surging toward Avalanche atop the Central

wbc.label: WBC — Final
wbc.note: Suárez 9th-inning double sealed it Tuesday · WBC stars return to spring camps this week

topStories.headline: Ovechkin scores No. 922 — one of the greatest nights in Washington sports history
body: Alex Ovechkin scored his 922nd career goal on Wednesday, deflecting a Rasmus Sandin centering pass off his right skate past Linus Ullmark to give Washington a 1–0 lead over Ottawa in the second period. It was his 25th goal of the season, tying Gordie Howe for the most 25-goal seasons in NHL history. The Capitals won 4–1. Ovechkin is now 78 goals from 1,000 career goals — a number that once seemed impossible and now seems inevitable, if his legs hold.
Adding to the magic: rookie Cole Hutson scored his first NHL goal in the same game, one of those rare nights where history and youth collide cleanly. The Capital One Arena was electric.

topStories.headline: Dallas stuns Colorado in a shootout — the Western Conference race just got interesting
body: Jake Oettinger made 33 saves and stopped all three shootout attempts as the Dallas Stars upset the Colorado Avalanche 2–1 in Denver on Wednesday night. Wyatt Johnston scored the lone shootout goal in round three to complete it. The Stars have now gone 15–1–1 in their last 17 games, a scorching run that has them within two points of Colorado atop the Central Division. The Avalanche's lead at the top of the West suddenly looks much less comfortable.

topStories.headline: Jaylen Brown is top 10 all-time in Celtics scoring — Boston closes the homestand in style
body: Jaylen Brown scored 32 points Wednesday, passing Dave Cowens to move into 10th on Boston's all-time scoring list. Jayson Tatum added 24 points and 10 rebounds in his third double-double in six games. The Celtics romped past Golden State 120–99 to sweep a three-game homestand. The Warriors, now 32–38, are out of the play-in picture entirely.

stories.headline: LeBron and Luka combine for 70 as Lakers extend streak to seven
body: LeBron James and Luka Doncic combined for 70 points as the Los Angeles Lakers won in Houston on Wednesday, matching their longest winning streak of the season at seven games. The Lakers are firmly in the West playoff picture with this run, and the James-Doncic pairing continues to find new gears late in the season. New Orleans also got a comfortable 124–109 road win over the Clippers, led by a quietly strong Pelicans squad trending up at the right time.

stories.headline: Celtics' Tatum back healthy — Warriors' Curry timeline still murky
body: Jayson Tatum played 34 minutes Wednesday with no restrictions, a good sign after the Achilles tendinopathy that had him questionable earlier in the week. Boston looks healthy entering the home stretch. On the other end, Stephen Curry's knee rehab has no firm return date. Golden State (32–38) has lost six straight and is officially a lottery team. The organization faces real questions about who's on the roster come October.

stories.headline: Dallas is on fire — 15-1-1 in 17 games, closing in on Colorado
body: The Dallas Stars are playing the best hockey in the Western Conference right now. Wednesday's shootout win in Colorado — where they outshot the Avalanche and Oettinger was a wall — extended a remarkable run. Jason Robertson scored in regulation. The Stars (43–15–10) are within two points of the Avalanche for the Central lead and have genuine Cup contender credentials. Colorado's margin for error just got thinner.

stories.headline: Crosby returns; Hurricanes survive a wild one in OT
body: Sidney Crosby returned to Pittsburgh's lineup Wednesday after missing time with an upper-body injury, and for most of the night it looked like a storybook comeback — the Penguins led 5–4 with under a minute left. Then Sean Walker scored with 28.3 seconds left to tie it, and Walker again delivered in overtime as Carolina won 6–5. The Hurricanes remain atop the Metropolitan Division. Pittsburgh got a great Crosby performance but nothing to show for it.

stories.headline: WBC stars trickle back to camps — Opening Night eight days away
body: Players from the World Baseball Classic are reporting to spring training sites this week, and MLB teams are balancing excitement with caution about workloads. Venezuela's Eugenio Suárez and Eduardo Rodriguez — the heroes of Tuesday's final — return to their respective clubs. Bryce Harper and Team USA stars head back to Phillies camp. The window to finalize 26-man rosters is shrinking fast with Opening Night on March 25.

One subplot worth watching: teams that sent multiple starters to the WBC — the Dodgers, Yankees, and Padres most notably — are managing pitch counts carefully. No one wants their ace arriving at the regular season gassed.
